translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the words which Moses spoke to all Israel on this side the Jordan, in the wilderness, in the plain, opposite to Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Dizahab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There are eleven days' journey from Horeb by the way of mount Seir to Kadesh-barnea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first of the month, that Moses spoke to the children of Israel, according to all that Jehovah had given him in command to them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: after he had smitten Sihon the king of the Amorites, who dwelt at Heshbon, and Og the king of Bashan, who dwelt at Ashtaroth [and] at Edrei.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On this side the Jordan, in the land of Moab, began Moses to unfold this law, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah ourGod spoke unto us in Horeb, saying, Ye have stayed long enough in this mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Turn and take your journey, and go to the hill-country of the Amorites, and unto all the neighbouring places in the plain, in the mountain, and in the lowland, and in the south, and by the seaside, the land of the Canaanites, and Lebanon, unto the great river, the river Euphrates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I have set the land before you: go in and possess the land which Jehovah swore unto your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give unto them and to their seed after them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I spoke unto you at that time, saying, I am not able to bear you myself alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah yourGod hath multiplied you, and behold, ye are this day as the stars of heaven for multitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, theGod of your fathers, make you a thousand times so many more as ye are, and bless you as he hath said unto you!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How can I myself alone sustain your wear, and your burden, and your strife?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Provide you wise and understanding and known men, according to your tribes, that I may make them your chiefs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye answered me, and said, The thing that thou hast spoken is good [for us] to do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So I took the chiefs of your tribes, wise men and known, and made them chiefs over you, captains of thousands, and captains of hundreds, and captains of fifties, and captains of tens, and officers for your tribes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I commanded your judges at that time, saying, Hear [the causes] between your brethren, and judge righteously between a man and his brother, and him also that sojourneth with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not respect persons in judgment: ye shall hear the small as well as the great; ye shall not be afraid of the face of man, for the judgment isGod's; and the matter that is too hard for you shall ye bring to me, that I may hear it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I commanded you at that time all the things that ye should do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we departed from Horeb and went through all that great and terrible wilderness, which ye saw, on the way to the mountain of the Amorites, as Jehovah ourGod had commanded us; and we came to Kadesh-barnea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said unto you, Ye are come unto the mountain of the Amorites, which Jehovah ourGod giveth us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, Jehovah thyGod hath set the land before thee: go up, take possession, as Jehovah theGod of thy fathers hath said unto thee; fear not, neither be dismayed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye came near to me all of you, and said, We will send men before us, who shall examine the land for us, and bring us word again of the way by which we must go up, and of the cities to which we shall come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the matter was good in mine eyes; and I took twelve men of you, one man for a tribe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they turned and went up into the mountain, and came to the valley of Eshcol, and searched it out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took of the fruit of the land in their hand, and brought it down unto us, and brought us answer, and said, The land is good that Jehovah ourGod hath given us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye would not go up, and rebelled against the word of Jehovah yourGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye murmured in your tents, and said, Because Jehovah hated us, he hath brought us forth out of the land of Egypt, to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whither shall we go up? Our brethren have made our hearts melt, saying, [They are] a people greater and taller than we; the cities are great and walled up to heaven; and moreover we have seen the sons of the Anakim there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said unto you, Be not afraid, neither fear them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah yourGod who goeth before you, he will fight for you, according to all that he did for you in Egypt before your eyes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in the wilderness where thou hast seen that Jehovah thyGod bore thee, as a man doth bear his son, in all the way that ye went, until ye came to this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But In this thing ye did not believe Jehovah yourGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who went in the way before you, to search you out a place for your encamping, in fire by night, to shew you by what way ye should go, and in the cloud by day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah heard the voice of your words, and was wroth, and swore, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: None among these men, this evil generation, shall in any wise see that good land, which I swore to give unto your fathers!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Except Caleb the son of Jephunneh, he shall see it, and to him will I give the land that he hath trodden upon, and to his children, because he hath wholly followed Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Also Jehovah was angry with me on your account, saying, Thou also shalt not go in thither.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Joshua the son of Nun, who standeth before thee, he shall go in thither: strengthen him, for he shall cause Israel to inherit it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And your little ones, of whom ye said, They shall be a prey, and your children, who this day know neither good nor evil, they shall go in thither, and unto them will I give it, and they shall possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye, turn, and take your journey into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And ye answered and said unto me, We have sinned against Jehovah, we will go up and fight, according to all that Jehovah ourGod hath commanded us. And ye girded on every man his weapons of war, and ye would go presumptuously up the hill.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to me, Say unto them, Go not up, neither fight; for I am not among you; lest ye be smitten before your enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I spoke unto you, but ye would not hear, and ye rebelled against the word of Jehovah, and acted presumptuously, and went up the hill.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Amorite that dwelt on that hill came out against you, and chased you, like as bees do, and cut you in pieces in Seir, as far as Hormah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye returned and wept before Jehovah, but Jehovah would not listen to your voice, nor give ear unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye abode in Kadesh many days, according unto the days that ye abode [there].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we turned, and took our journey into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea, as Jehovah had said unto me; and we went round mount Seir many days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to me, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have gone round this mountain long enough: turn you northward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And command the people, saying, Ye are to pass through the border of your brethren the children of Esau, who dwell in Seir; and they will be afraid of you; and ye shall be very guarded:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: attack them not; for I will not give you of their land, no, not so much as a foot-breadth; for I have given mount Seir as a possession unto Esau.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall buy of them food for money, that ye may eat; and water shall ye also buy of them for money, that ye may drink;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Jehovah thyGod hath blessed thee in all the work of thy hand. He hath known thy walking through this great wilderness: these forty years hath Jehovah thyGod been with thee; thou hast lacked nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we passed by from our brethren the children of Esau, who dwelt in Seir, by the plain, by Elath, and by Ezion-geber, and we turned and passed by the way of the wilderness of Moab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to me, Distress not the Moabites, neither engage with them in battle; for I will not give thee of their land a possession; for unto the children of Lot have I given Ar as a possession.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (The Emim dwelt therein in times past, a people great, and many, and tall as the Anakim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They also are reckoned as giants like the Anakim; but the Moabites call them Emim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in Seir dwelt the Horites in times past; and the children of Esau dispossessed them, and destroyed them from before them, and dwelt in their stead; as Israel did to the land of their possession, which Jehovah gave to them.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now rise up, and pass over the torrent Zered. And we passed over the torrent Zered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the days in which we came from Kadesh-barnea, until we had come over the torrent Zered, were thirty-eight years; until the whole generation of the men of war was consumed from the midst of the camp, as Jehovah had sworn unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover the hand of Jehovah was against them to destroy them from the midst of the camp, until they were consumed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when all the men of war were consumed, having died off from among the people,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that Jehovah spoke to me, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou art to pass this day over the border of Moab, [which is] Ar,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and come near over against the children of Ammon; thou shalt not distress them nor attack them; for I will not give thee of the land of the children of Ammon a possession; for unto the children of Lot have I given it as a possession.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (That also is reckoned a land of giants: giants dwelt therein in time past, and the Ammonites call them Zamzummim;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a people great, and many, and tall as the Anakim; and Jehovah destroyed them before them, and they dispossessed them, and dwelt in their stead;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as he did to the children of Esau, who dwelt in Seir, from before whom he destroyed the Horites; and they dispossessed them, and dwelt in their stead, even to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Avvites who dwelt in the hamlets as far as Gazah — the Caphtorim, who came out of Caphtor, destroyed them, and dwelt in their stead.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rise up, take your journey, and pass over the river Arnon. Behold, I have given into thy hand Sihon the king of Heshbon, the Amorite, and his land: begin, take possession, and engage with him in battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This day will I begin to put the dread of thee and the fear of thee upon the peoples under the whole heaven; who will hear report of thee, and will tremble, and quake because of thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I sent messengers out of the wilderness of Kedemoth unto Sihon the king of Heshbon with words of peace, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let me pass through thy land: by the highway alone will I go; I will neither turn to the right hand nor to the left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt sell me food for money that I may eat; and thou shalt give me water for money that I may drink; I will only pass through on my feet,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — as the children of Esau who dwell in Seir, and the Moabites who dwell in Ar, did to me, — until I shall pass over the Jordan into the land which Jehovah ourGod giveth us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Sihon the king of Heshbon would not let us pass by him; for Jehovah thyGod hardened his spirit, and made his heart obdurate, that he might give him into thy hand, as it is this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to me, Behold, I begin to give Sihon and his land before thee: begin, take possession, that thou mayest possess his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Sihon came out against us for battle, he and all his people, to Jahaz.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah ourGod gave him up before us; and we smote him, and his sons, and his whole people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we took all his cities at that time, and utterly destroyed every city, men, and women, and little ones: we let none escape.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only the cattle we took as booty for ourselves, and the spoil of the cities which we took.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From Aroer, which is on the bank of the river Arnon, and the city that is in the ravine even to Gilead, there was not one city too strong for us: Jehovah ourGod delivered all before us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only thou didst not approach the land of the children of Ammon, the whole border of the river Jabbok, nor the cities of the mountain, nor to whatsoever Jehovah ourGod had forbidden us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we turned, and went up the way to Bashan; and Og the king of Bashan came out against us, he and all his people, for battle at Edrei.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to me, Fear him not; for into thy hand have I given him, and all his people, and his land; and thou shalt do unto him as thou didst unto Sihon the king of the Amorites, who dwelt at Heshbon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah ourGod gave into our hand Og the king of Bashan also, and all his people; and we smote him until none was left to him remaining.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we took all his cities at that time: there was not a town which we took not from them, sixty cities, the whole region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All these cities were fortified with high walls, gates, and bars; besides unwalled towns very many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we utterly destroyed them, as we had done to Sihon the king of Heshbon, utterly destroying every city, men, women and little ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But all the cattle and the spoil of the cities we took as booty for ourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we took at that time the land out of the hand of the two kings of the Amorites, that were on this side the Jordan, from the river Arnon to mount Hermon

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (the Sidonians call Hermon Sirion, and the Amorites call it Senir):

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all the cities of the plateau, and all Gilead, and all Bashan, as far as Salchah and Edrei, the cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For only Og the king of Bashan remained of the residue of giants: behold, his bedstead was a bedstead of iron; is it not in Rabbah of the children of Ammon? its length was nine cubits, and its breadth four cubits, after the cubit of a man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this land we took in possession at that time. From Aroer, which is by the river Arnon, and the half of mount Gilead, and its cities, I gave to the Reubenites and to the Gadites;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the rest of Gilead, and all Bashan, the kingdom of Og, I gave to half the tribe of Manasseh. (The whole region of Argob, even all Bashan, is called a land of giants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jair the son of Manasseh took the whole region of Argob as far as the border of the Geshurites and Maachathites, and called Bashan after his own name, Havoth-Jair, to this day.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I gave Gilead to Machir.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to the Reubenites and to the Gadites I gave from Gilead even to the river Arnon, the middle of the ravine and its border, as far as the river Jabbok, the border of the children of Ammon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the plain also, and the Jordan, and [its] border from Chinnereth as far as the sea of the plain, the salt sea, under the slopes of Pisgah eastward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I commanded you at that time, saying, Jehovah yourGod hath given you this land to take possession of it: ye shall pass over armed before your brethren the children of Israel, all [who are] combatants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only your wives, and your little ones, and your cattle, — I know that ye have much cattle, — shall abide in your cities which I have given you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: until Jehovah give rest to your brethren, as well as to you, and they also take possession of the land that Jehovah yourGod giveth them beyond the Jordan; then shall ye return, each man to his possession, which I have given you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I commanded Joshua at that time, saying, Thine eyes have seen all that Jehovah yourGod hath done to these two kings: so will Jehovah do to all the kingdoms to which thou shalt go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not fear them; for Jehovah yourGod, he will fight for you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I besought Jehovah at that time, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lord Jehovah, thou hast begun to shew thy servant thy greatness, and thy powerful hand; for whatGod is in the heavens or in the earth that can do like to thy works, and like to thy might?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let me go over, I pray thee, and see the good land that is beyond the Jordan, that goodly mountain, and Lebanon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah was wroth with me on your account, and did not hear me; and Jehovah said to me, Let it suffice thee; speak no more unto me of this matter!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go up to the top of Pisgah, and lift up thine eyes westward, and northward, and southward, and eastward, and behold it with thine eyes; for thou shalt not go over this Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But charge Joshua, and encourage him and strengthen him; for he shall go over before this people, and he shall put them in possession of the land which thou shalt see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we abode in the valley opposite to Beth-Peor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, Israel, hearken to the statutes and to the ordinances which I teach you, to do [them], that ye may live, and go in and possess the land which Jehovah theGod of your fathers giveth you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not add to the word which I command you, neither shall ye take from it, that ye may keep the commandments of Jehovah yourGod which I command you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Your eyes have seen what Jehovah did because of Baal-Peor; for all the men that followed Baal-Peor, Jehovah thyGod hath destroyed them from among you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but ye that did cleave to Jehovah yourGod are alive every one of you this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See, I have taught you statutes and ordinances, even as Jehovah myGod commanded me, that ye may do so in the land into which ye enter to possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall keep and do them; for that will be your wisdom and your understanding before the eyes of the peoples that shall hear all these statutes, and say, Verily this great nation is a wise and understanding people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For what great nation is there that hathGod near to them as Jehovah ourGod is in everything we call upon him for?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And what great nation is there that hath righteous statutes and ordinances, as all this law which I set before you this day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only take heed to thyself, and keep thy soul diligently, lest thou forget the things that thine eyes have seen (and lest they depart from thy heart all the days of thy life; but thou shalt make them known to thy sons and to thy sons' sons),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the day that thou stoodest before Jehovah thyGod in Horeb, when Jehovah said to me, Gather me the people together, that I may cause them to hear my words, that they may learn them, and fear me all the days that they live upon the earth, and teach them to their children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye came near and stood under the mountain; and the mountain burned with fire to the heart of heaven, with darkness, clouds, and obscurity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to you from the midst of the fire: ye heard the voice of the words, but ye saw no form; only [ye heard] a voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he declared to you his covenant, which he commanded you to do, the ten words; and he wrote them on two tables of stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah commanded me at that time to teach you statutes and ordinances, that ye might do them in the land whither ye are passing over to possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And take great heed to your souls (for ye saw no form on the day that Jehovah spoke to you in Horeb from the midst of the fire),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest ye corrupt yourselves, and make you a graven image, the form of any figure, the pattern of male or female,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the pattern of any beast that is on the earth, the pattern of any winged fowl that flieth in the heaven,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the pattern of anything that creepeth on the ground, the pattern of any fish that is in the waters under the earth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and lest thou lift up thine eyes to the heavens, and see the sun, and the moon, and the stars, the whole host of heaven, and be drawn away and bow down to them and serve them, which Jehovah thyGod hath assigned unto all peoples under the whole heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But you hath Jehovah taken, and hath brought you forth out of the iron furnace, out of Egypt, that ye might be to him a people of inheritance, as it is this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah was angry with me on your account, and swore that I should not go over the Jordan, and that I should not enter in to that good land which Jehovah thyGod giveth thee [for] an inheritance;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I shall die in this land, I shall not go over the Jordan; but ye shall go over, and possess this good land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed to yourselves lest ye forget the covenant of Jehovah yourGod, which he made with you, and make yourselves a graven image, the form of anything which Jehovah thyGod hath forbidden thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah thyGod is a consuming fire, a jealousGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thou begettest sons, and sons' sons, and ye have remained long in the land, and shall corrupt yourselves, and make a graven image, the form of anything, and do evil in the sight of Jehovah thyGod, to provoke him to anger,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I call heaven and earth to witness against you this day, that ye shall soon utterly perish from off the land whereunto ye pass over the Jordan to possess it: ye shall not prolong your days on it, but shall be utterly destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will scatter you among the peoples, and ye shall be left a small company among the nations to which Jehovah will lead you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall there servegods, the work of men's hands, wood and stone, which neither see, nor hear, nor eat, nor smell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And from thence ye shall seek Jehovah thyGod, and thou shalt find him, if thou shalt seek him with thy whole heart and with thy whole soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In thy tribulation, and when all these things shall come upon thee, at the end of days, thou shalt return to Jehovah thyGod, and shalt hearken to his voice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — for Jehovah thyGod is a mercifulGod, — he will not forsake thee, neither destroy thee, nor forget the covenant of thy fathers which he swore unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ask now of the days that are past, which were before thee, since the day thatGod created man on the earth, and from one end of the heavens to the other end of the heavens, whether there hath been anything as this great thing is, or if anything hath been heard like it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Did [ever] people hear the voice ofGod speaking from the midst of the fire, as thou hast heard, and live?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or hathGod essayed to come to take him a nation from the midst of a nation, by trials, by signs, and by wonders, and by war, and by a powerful hand, and by a stretched-out arm, and by great terrors, according to all that Jehovah yourGod did for you in Egypt before your eyes?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unto thee it was shewn, that thou mightest know that Jehovah, he isGod — there is none other besides him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the heavens he made thee hear his voice, that he might instruct thee; and on the earth he shewed thee his great fire; and thou heardest his words from the midst of the fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And because he loved thy fathers, and chose their seed after them, he brought thee out with his countenance, with his great power, out of Egypt,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to dispossess nations from before thee greater and mightier than thou art, to bring thee in, to give thee their land for an inheritance, as it is this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt know therefore this day, and consider it in thy heart, that Jehovah, he isGod in the heavens above, and on the earth beneath: [there is] none else.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt keep his statutes, and his commandments, which I command thee this day, that it may be well with thee and with thy sons after thee, and that thou mayest prolong thy days on the land which Jehovah thyGod giveth thee, for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Moses separated three cities on this side the Jordan toward the sun-rising,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that the manslayer might flee thither, who should kill his neighbour unawares, and hated him not previously, that fleeing to one of these cities, he might live:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bezer in the wilderness, in the plateau, of the Reubenites, and Ramoth in Gilead, of the Gadites, and Golan in Bashan, of the Manassites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the law which Moses set before the children of Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: these are the testimonies, and the statutes, and the ordinances that Moses declared to the children of Israel, when they came out of Egypt,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: on this side the Jordan, in the valley opposite to Beth-Peor, in the land of Sihon the king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon, whom Moses and the children of Israel smote when they came out of Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they took possession of his land, and the land of Og the king of Bashan, two kings of the Amorites, who were on this side the Jordan, toward the sun-rising;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from Aroer, which is on the bank of the river Arnon, as far as mount Sion, which is Hermon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the plain on this side the Jordan, eastward, and as far as the sea of the plain, under the slopes of Pisgah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses called to all Israel, and said to them, Hear, Israel, the statutes and the ordinances that I speak in your ears this day, and learn them, and keep them to do them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah ourGod made a covenant with us in Horeb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Not with our fathers did Jehovah make this covenant, but with us, [even] us, those [who are] here alive all of us this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Face to face on the mountain from the midst of the fire Jehovah spoke with you

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (I stood between Jehovah and you at that time, to declare to you the word of Jehovah; for ye were afraid by reason of the fire, and went not up to the mountain), saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am Jehovah thyGod who have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt have no othergods before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not make thyself any graven image, any form of what is in the heavens above, or what is in the earth beneath, or what is in the waters under the earth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them; for I, Jehovah thyGod, am a jealousGod, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the sons, and upon the third and upon the fourth [generation] of them that hate me,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shewing mercy unto thousands of them that love me and keep my commandments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not idly utter the name of Jehovah thyGod; for Jehovah will not hold him guiltless that idly uttereth his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep the sabbath day to hallow it, as Jehovah thyGod hath commanded thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the seventh day is the sabbath of Jehovah thyGod: thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, nor thy bondman, nor thy handmaid, nor thine ox, nor thine ass, nor any of thy cattle, nor thy sojourner that is within thy gates; that thy bondman and thy handmaid may rest as well as thou.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt remember that thou wast a bondman in the land of Egypt, and that Jehovah thyGod brought thee out thence with a powerful hand and with a stretched-out arm; therefore Jehovah thyGod hath commanded thee to observe the sabbath day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Honour thy father and thy mother, as Jehovah thyGod hath commanded thee; that thy days may be prolonged, and that it may be well with thee in the land which Jehovah thyGod giveth thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not kill.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither shalt thou commit adultery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither shalt thou steal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither shalt thou bear false witness against thy neighbour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither shalt thou desire thy neighbour's wife, neither shalt thou covet thy neighbour's house, his field, nor his bondman, nor his handmaid, his ox, nor his ass, nor anything that is thy neighbour's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These words Jehovah spoke to all your congregation on the mountain from the midst of the fire, of the cloud, and of the obscurity, with a great voice, and he added no more; and he wrote them on two tables of stone, and gave them to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when ye heard the voice from the midst of the darkness, and the mountain burned with fire, that ye came near to me, all the heads of your tribes, and your elders;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye said, Behold, Jehovah ourGod has shewn us his glory and his greatness, and we have heard his voice from the midst of the fire: we have seen this day thatGod talks with man, and he lives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, why should we die? for this great fire will consume us. If we hear the voice of Jehovah ourGod any more, we shall die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For who is there of all flesh, that has heard the voice of the livingGod speaking from the midst of the fire, as we, and has lived?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come thou near, and hear all that Jehovah ourGod will say; and speak thou to us all that Jehovah ourGod will speak to thee; and we will hear it, and do it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah heard the voice of your words, when ye spoke to me; and Jehovah said unto me, I have heard the voice of the words of this people that have spoken to thee: they have well spoken all that they have spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that there were such a heart in them, that they would fear me, and keep all my commandments continually, that it might be well with them and with their sons for ever!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go, say unto them, Get you into your tents again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for thee, stand thou here by me, and I will speak unto thee all the commandments, and the statutes, and the ordinances, which thou shalt teach them, that they may do them in the land which I give them to possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed then to do as Jehovah yourGod hath commanded you: turn not aside to the right hand or to the left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In all the way that Jehovah yourGod hath commanded you shall ye walk, that ye may live, and that it may be well with you, and that ye may prolong your days in the land which ye shall possess.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the commandments, the statutes, and the ordinances, which Jehovah yourGod commanded to teach you, that ye may do them in the land whereunto ye pass over to possess it,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that thou mayest fear Jehovah thyGod, to keep all his statutes and his commandments which I command thee, thou, and thy son, and thy son's son, all the days of thy life; and that thy days may be prolonged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt hear, Israel, and take heed to do [them]; that it may be well with thee, and that ye may increase greatly, as Jehovah theGod of thy fathers hath said unto thee, in a land flowing with milk and honey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, Israel: Jehovah ourGod is one Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt love Jehovah thyGod with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these words, which I command thee this day, shall be in thy heart;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt impress them on thy sons, and shalt talk of them when thou sittest in thy house, and when thou goest on the way, and when thou liest down, and when thou risest up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt bind them for a sign on thy hand, and they shall be for frontlets between thine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt write them upon the posts of thy house, and upon thy gates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, when Jehovah thyGod bringeth thee into the land which he swore unto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give thee: great and good cities which thou buildedst not,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and houses full of everything good which thou filledst not, and wells digged which thou diggedst not, vineyards and oliveyards which thou plantedst not, and thou shalt have eaten and shalt be full;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [then] beware lest thou forget Jehovah who brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt fear Jehovah thyGod, and serve him, and shalt swear by his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not go after othergods, of thegods of the peoples that are round about you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Jehovah thyGod is a jealousGod in thy midst; lest the anger of Jehovah thyGod be kindled against thee, and he destroy thee from the face of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not tempt Jehovah yourGod, as ye tempted him in Massah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall diligently keep the commandments of Jehovah yourGod, and his testimonies, and his statutes, which he hath commanded thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt do what is right and good in the sight of Jehovah, that it may be well with thee, and that thou mayest enter in and possess the good land which Jehovah swore unto thy fathers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thrusting out all thine enemies from before thee, as Jehovah hath spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thy son shall ask thee in time to come, saying, What are the testimonies, and the statutes, and the ordinances, which Jehovah ourGod hath commanded you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then thou shalt say unto thy son, We were Pharaoh's bondmen in Egypt; and Jehovah brought us out of Egypt with a powerful hand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jehovah shewed signs and wonders, great and grievous, upon Egypt, upon Pharaoh, and upon all his household, before our eyes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he brought us out thence, that he might bring us in, to give us the land which he swore unto our fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah commanded us to do all these statutes, to fear Jehovah ourGod, for our good continually, that he might preserve us alive, as it is this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be our righteousness if we take heed to do all these commandments before Jehovah ourGod, as he hath commanded us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Jehovah thyGod shall bring thee into the land whither thou goest to possess it, and shall cast out many nations from before thee, the Hittites, and the Girgashites, and the Amorites, and the Canaanites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, seven nations greater and mightier than thou,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and when Jehovah thyGod shall give them up before thee and thou shalt smite them, then shalt thou utterly destroy them: thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor shew mercy unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make no marriages with them: thy daughter thou shalt not give unto his son, nor take his daughter for thy son;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he will turn away thy son from following me, and they will serve othergods, and the anger of Jehovah will be kindled against you, and he will destroy thee quickly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thus shall ye deal with them: ye shall break down their altars, and shatter their statues, and hew down their Asherahs, and burn their graven images with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a holy people art thou unto Jehovah thyGod: Jehovah thyGod hath chosen thee to be unto him a people for a possession, above all the peoples that are upon the face of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Not because ye were more in number than all the peoples, hath Jehovah been attached to you and chosen you, for ye are the fewest of all the peoples;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but because Jehovah loved you, and because he would keep the oath which he had sworn unto your fathers, hath Jehovah brought you out with a powerful hand, and redeemed you out of the house of bondage, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt know that Jehovah thyGod, he isGod, the faithfulGod, who keepeth covenant and mercy to a thousand generations with them that love him and keep his commandments;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and repayeth them that hate him [each] to his face, to cause them to perish: he delayeth not with him that hateth him, he will repay him to his face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt keep the commandment, and the statutes, and the ordinances, which I command thee this day, to do them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, if ye hearken to these ordinances, and keep and do them, that Jehovah thyGod will keep with thee the covenant and the mercy which he swore unto thy fathers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he will love thee, and bless thee, and multiply thee, and will bless the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy ground, thy corn and thy new wine, and thine oil, the offspring of thy kine, and the increase of thy sheep, in the land which he swore unto thy fathers to give thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt be blessed above all the peoples; there shall not be male or female barren with thee, or with thy cattle;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jehovah will take away from thee all sickness, and none of the evil infirmities of Egypt, which thou knowest, will he put upon thee; but he will lay them upon all them that hate thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt consume all the peoples that Jehovah thyGod will give up unto thee; thine eye shall not spare them, and thou shalt not serve theirgods; for that would be a snare unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou shouldest say in thy heart, These nations are greater than I; how can I dispossess them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: fear them not; remember well what Jehovah thyGod did unto Pharaoh, and unto all the Egyptians;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the great trials which thine eyes saw, and the signs, and the wonders, and the powerful hand, and the stretched-out arm, whereby Jehovah thyGod brought thee out: so will Jehovah thyGod do unto all the peoples whom thou fearest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover, Jehovah thyGod will send the hornet among them, until they that are left, and they that hide themselves from thee, are destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not be afraid of them; for Jehovah thyGod is in thy midst, aGod great and terrible.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah thyGod will cast out those nations from before thee by little and little; thou shalt not be able to make an end of them at once, lest the beasts of the field increase upon thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah thyGod will give them up before thee, and will confound them with great consternation, until they are destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he will give their kings into thy hand, and thou shalt put out their name from under the heavens; no man shall stand before thee, until thou hast destroyed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The graven images of theirgods shall ye burn with fire; thou shalt not covet the silver and gold [that is] on them and take it unto thee, lest thou be ensnared therein; for it is an abomination to Jehovah thyGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt not bring an abomination into thy house, lest thou be a cursed thing like it: thou shalt utterly detest it, and thou shalt utterly abhor it; for it is a cursed thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every commandment which I command thee this day shall ye take heed to do, that ye may live, and multiply, and enter in and possess the land which Jehovah swore unto your fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt remember all the way which Jehovah thyGod led thee these forty years in the wilderness, to humble thee, and to prove thee, to know what was in thy heart, whether thou wouldest keep his commandments or not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he humbled thee, and suffered thee to hunger, and fed thee with the manna, which thou hadst not known, and which thy fathers knew not; that he might make thee know that man doth not live by bread alone, but by everything that goeth out of the mouth of Jehovah doth man live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy clothing grew not old upon thee, neither did thy foot swell, these forty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And know in thy heart that, as a man chasteneth his son, so Jehovah thyGod chasteneth thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt keep the commandments of Jehovah thyGod, to walk in his ways, and to fear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah thyGod bringeth thee into a good land, a land of water-brooks, of springs, and of deep waters, that gush forth in the valleys and hills;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a land of wheat, and barley, and vines, and fig-trees, and pomegranates; a land of olive-trees and honey;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a land wherein thou shalt eat bread without scarceness, where thou shalt lack nothing; a land whose stones are iron, and out of whose mountains thou wilt dig copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt eat and be filled, and shalt bless Jehovah thyGod for the good land which he hath given thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Beware that thou forget not Jehovah thyGod, in not keeping his commandments, and his ordinances, and his statutes, which I command thee this day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest when thou hast eaten and art full, and hast built and inhabited fine houses,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thy herds and thy flocks multiply, and thy silver and thy gold is multiplied, and all that thou hast is multiplied,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then thy heart be lifted up, and thou forget Jehovah thyGod, who brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who led thee through the great and terrible wilderness, [a wilderness of] fiery serpents, and scorpions, and drought, where there is no water; who brought thee forth water out of the rock of flint;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who fed thee in the wilderness with manna, which thy fathers knew not, that he might humble thee, and that he might prove thee, to do thee good at thy latter end;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — and thou say in thy heart, My power and the might of my hand has procured me this wealth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou shalt remember Jehovah thyGod, that it is he who giveth thee power to get wealth, that he may establish his covenant which he swore unto thy fathers, as it is this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, if thou do at all forget Jehovah thyGod, and go after othergods, and serve them, and bow down to them, I testify against you this day that ye shall utterly perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As the nations which Jehovah is causing to perish before you, so shall ye perish; because ye would not hearken unto the voice of Jehovah yourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, Israel! Thou art to pass over the Jordan this day, to enter in to possess nations greater and mightier than thou, cities great and walled up to heaven,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a people great and tall, the sons of the Anakim, whom thou knowest, and of whom thou hast heard [say], Who can stand before the sons of Anak!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Know then this day, that Jehovah thyGod is he that goeth over before thee, a consuming fire; he will destroy them, and he will cast them down before thee, and thou shalt dispossess them and cause them to perish quickly, as Jehovah hath said unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not say in thy heart, when Jehovah thyGod thrusteth them out from before thee, saying, For my righteousness Jehovah hath brought me in to possess this land; but for the wickedness of these nations doth Jehovah dispossess them from before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Not for thy righteousness, or for the uprightness of thy heart, dost thou enter in to possess their land, but for the wickedness of these nations doth Jehovah thyGod dispossess them from before thee, and that he may perform the word which Jehovah swore unto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Know therefore that Jehovah thyGod doth not give thee this good land to possess it for thy righteousness; for thou art a stiff-necked people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember, forget not, how thou provokedst Jehovah thyGod to wrath in the wilderness. From the day that thou didst depart out of the land of Egypt, until ye came to this place, ye have been rebellious against Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And at Horeb ye provoked Jehovah to wrath, and Jehovah was angry with you, to destroy you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when I went up the mountain to receive the tables of stone, the tables of the covenant which Jehovah made with you, and I abode in the mountain forty days and forty nights, — I ate no bread and drank no water, —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — and Jehovah delivered to me the two tables of stone written with the finger ofGod; and on them [was written] according to all the words which Jehovah spoke with you on the mountain from the midst of the fire on the day of the assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of forty days and forty nights, that Jehovah gave me the two tables of stone, the tables of the covenant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, Arise, go down quickly from hence; for thy people which thou hast brought forth out of Egypt have corrupted themselves; they have quickly turned aside from the way which I commanded them: they have made for themselves a molten image.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke unto me, saying, I have seen this people, and behold, it is a stiff-necked people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let me alone, that I may destroy them, and blot out their name from under heaven; and I will make of thee a nation mightier and greater than they.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I turned and came down from the mountain, and the mountain burned with fire; and the two tables of the covenant were in my two hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I saw, and behold, ye had sinned against Jehovah yourGod: ye had made for yourselves a molten calf; ye had quickly turned aside from the way which Jehovah had commanded you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I seized the two tables, and cast them out of my two hands, and broke them before your eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I fell down before Jehovah, as at the first, forty days and forty nights, — I ate no bread and drank no water, — because of all your sin which ye had sinned, in doing what is evil in the eyes of Jehovah, to provoke him to anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I was afraid of the anger and fury wherewith Jehovah was wroth against you to destroy you. And Jehovah listened unto me also at that time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And with Aaron Jehovah was very angry to destroy him; and I prayed for Aaron also at the same time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I took your sin, the calf which ye had made, and burned it with fire, and crushed it, and ground it very small, until it became fine dust; and I cast the dust thereof into the brook that flowed down from the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And at Taberah, and at Massah, and at Kibroth-hattaavah, ye provoked Jehovah to wrath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jehovah sent you from Kadesh-barnea, saying, Go up and take possession of the land which I have given you, ye rebelled against the word of Jehovah yourGod, and ye believed him not, nor hearkened to his voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have been rebellious against Jehovah from the day that I knew you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So I fell down before Jehovah the forty days and forty nights, as I fell down; for Jehovah had said he would destroy you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I prayed therefore to Jehovah, and said, Lord Jehovah, destroy not thy people and thine inheritance, which thou hast redeemed through thy greatness, which thou hast brought forth out of Egypt with a powerful hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember thy servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; look not at the stubbornness of this people, nor at their wickedness, nor at their sin;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest the land whence thou broughtest us out say, Because Jehovah was not able to bring them into the land which he had promised them, and because he hated them, he hath brought them out to kill them in the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are indeed thy people and thine inheritance, which thou broughtest out with thy great power and with thy stretched-out arm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time Jehovah said unto me, Hew for thyself two tables of stone like the first, and come up unto me into the mountain, and make thee an ark of wood;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will write on the tables the words that were on the first tables which thou didst break, and thou shalt lay them in the ark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I made an ark of acacia-wood, and hewed two tables of stone like the first, and went up the mountain with the two tables in my hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he wrote on the tables, as the first writing, the ten words which Jehovah spoke unto you on the mountain, from the midst of the fire, on the day of the assembly, and Jehovah gave them unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I turned and came down from the mountain, and put the tables in the ark which I had made; — and they are there, as Jehovah commanded me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (And the children of Israel took their journey from Beeroth-Bene-Jaakan to Moserah: there Aaron died, and there he was buried; and Eleazar his son exercised the priesthood in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From thence they journeyed unto Gudgodah; and from Gudgodah to Jotbathah, a land of water-brooks.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time Jehovah separated the tribe of Levi, to bear the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, to stand before Jehovah to do service unto him, and to bless in his name, unto this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore Levi has no portion nor inheritance with his brethren; Jehovah is his inheritance, according as Jehovah thyGod told him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I stood upon the mountain according to the former days, forty days and forty nights; and Jehovah listened unto me also at that time: Jehovah would not destroy thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto me, Rise up, take thy journey before the people, that they may enter in and possess the land, which I swore unto their fathers to give unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, Israel, what doth Jehovah thyGod require of thee, but to fear Jehovah thyGod, to walk in all his ways, and to love him, and to serve Jehovah thyGod with all thy heart and with all thy soul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to keep the commandments of Jehovah, and his statutes, which I command thee this day, for thy good?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, the heaven and the heaven of heavens belong to Jehovah thyGod; the earth and all that is therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only, Jehovah took pleasure in thy fathers, to love them, and he chose their seed after them, [even] you, out of all the peoples, as it is this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Circumcise then the foreskin of your heart, and stiffen your neck no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah yourGod is theGod ofgods, and the Lord of lords, the greatGod, the mighty and the terrible, who regardeth not persons, nor taketh reward;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who executeth the judgment of the fatherless and the widow, and loveth the stranger, to give him food and clothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall love the stranger; for ye have been strangers in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt fear Jehovah thyGod; him thou shalt serve, and unto him shalt thou cleave, and swear by his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is thy praise, and he is thyGod, who hath done for thee these great and terrible things, which thine eyes have seen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With seventy souls thy fathers went down into Egypt; and now Jehovah thyGod hath made thee as the stars of heaven for multitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt love then Jehovah thyGod, and keep his charge, and his statutes, and his ordinances, and his commandments continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And know ye this day...; for [I speak] not with your children who have not known, and who have not seen the chastisement of Jehovah yourGod, his greatness, his powerful hand, and his stretched-out arm,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his signs and his acts which he did in the midst of Egypt unto Pharaoh the king of Egypt, and unto all his land;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and what he did unto the army of Egypt, unto their horses, and unto their chariots, over which he made the water of the Red sea flow as they pursued after you, and Jehovah destroyed them unto this day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — and what he did unto you in the wilderness, until ye came to this place;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and what he did unto Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab, the son of Reuben, — how the earth opened its mouth, and swallowed them up, with their households, and their tents, and all the living substance that belonged to them, in the midst of all Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For your eyes have seen all the great work of Jehovah which he hath done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep then all the commandment which I command you this day, that ye may be strong, and enter in and possess the land, whither ye pass over to possess it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and that ye may prolong your days in the land which Jehovah swore unto your fathers to give unto them and unto their seed, a land flowing with milk and honey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the land, whither thou enterest in to possess it, is not as the land of Egypt, from whence ye came out, where thou sowedst thy seed, and wateredst it with thy foot, as a garden of herbs;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the land, whereunto ye are passing over to possess it, is a land of mountains and valleys, which drinketh water of the rain of heaven,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a land which Jehovah thyGod careth for; the eyes of Jehovah thyGod are constantly upon it, from the beginning of the year even unto the end of the year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, if ye hearken diligently unto my commandments which I command you this day, to love Jehovah yourGod, and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that I will give rain to your land in its season, the early rain and the latter rain; and thou shalt gather in thy corn, and thy new wine, and thine oil;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will give grass in thy field for thy cattle; and thou shalt eat and be full.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed to yourselves, that your heart be not deceived, and ye turn aside and serve othergods, and bow down to them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jehovah's wrath kindle against you, and he shut up the heavens, that there be no rain, and that the ground yield not its produce, and ye perish quickly from off the good land which Jehovah is giving you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall lay up these my words in your heart and in your soul, and bind them for a sign upon your hand, and they shall be for frontlets between your eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall teach them unto your children, speaking of them when thou sittest in thy house, and when thou goest on the way, and when thou liest down, and when thou risest up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and write them upon the posts of thy house, and upon thy gates;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that your days may be multiplied, and the days of your children, in the land which Jehovah swore unto your fathers to give them, as the days of the heavens [which are] above the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if ye diligently keep all this commandment which I command you [this day] to do it, to love Jehovah yourGod, to walk in all his ways, and to cleave unto him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then will Jehovah dispossess all these nations from before you, and ye shall take possession of nations greater and mightier than yourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every place whereon the sole of your foot shall tread shall be yours; from the wilderness and Lebanon, from the river, the river Euphrates, even unto the hinder sea shall your border be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No man shall be able to stand before you: the fear of you and the dread of you will Jehovah yourGod lay upon all the land that ye shall tread upon, as he hath said unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See, I set before you this day a blessing and a curse:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a blessing, if ye obey the commandments of Jehovah yourGod, which I command you this day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and a curse, if ye will not obey the commandments of Jehovah yourGod, but turn aside out of the way which I command you this day, to go after othergods which ye have not known.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when Jehovah thyGod hath brought thee into the land whither thou enterest in to possess it, that thou shalt put the blessing upon mount Gerizim, and the curse upon mount Ebal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are they not on the other side of the Jordan, beyond the way toward the going down of the sun, in the land of the Canaanites that dwell on the plain opposite to Gilgal, beside the oaks of Moreh?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye pass over the Jordan to enter in to possess the land which Jehovah yourGod giveth you, and ye shall take possession of it, and dwell therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall take heed to do all the statutes and ordinances which I set before you this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the statutes and ordinances, which ye shall take heed to do in the land, which Jehovah theGod of thy fathers is giving thee to possess it, all the days that ye live upon the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall utterly destroy all the places wherein the nations which ye shall dispossess have served theirgods, upon the high mountains, and upon the hills, and under every green tree;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye shall break down their altars, and shatter their statues, and burn their Asherahs with fire; and ye shall hew down the graven images of theirgods, and ye shall destroy the names of them out of that place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not do so unto Jehovah yourGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but unto the place which Jehovah yourGod will choose out of all your tribes to set his name there, his habitation shall ye seek, and thither thou shalt come;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thither ye shall bring your burnt-offerings and your sacrifices, and your tithes, and the heave-offering of your hand, and your vows, and your voluntary-offerings, and the firstlings of your kine and of your sheep;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye shall eat there before Jehovah yourGod, and ye shall rejoice, ye and your households, in all the business of your hand, wherein Jehovah thyGod hath blessed thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not do after all that we do here this day, each one whatever is right in his own eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye are not as yet come to the rest and to the inheritance which Jehovah thyGod giveth thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when ye have gone over the Jordan, and dwell in the land which Jehovah yourGod causeth you to inherit, and when he hath given you rest from all your enemies round about, and ye dwell in safety,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then there shall be a place which Jehovah yourGod will choose to cause his name to dwell there; thither shall ye bring all that I command you: your burnt-offerings, and your sacrifices, your tithes, and the heave-offering of your hand, and all your choice vows which ye shall vow to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall rejoice before Jehovah yourGod, ye, and your sons, and your daughters, and your bondmen, and your handmaids, and the Levite that is within your gates; for he hath no portion nor inheritance with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed to thyself that thou offer not thy burnt-offerings in every place that thou seest;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but in the place which Jehovah will choose in one of thy tribes, there thou shalt offer thy burnt-offerings, and there thou shalt do all that I command thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless, according to all the desire of thy soul thou mayest slay and eat flesh in all thy gates, according to the blessing of Jehovah thyGod which he hath given thee: the unclean and the clean may eat thereof, as of the gazelle, and the hart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only, ye shall not eat the blood; ye shall pour it upon the earth as water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou mayest not eat within thy gates the tithe of thy corn, or of thy new wine, or of thine oil, or the firstlings of thy kine or of thy sheep, nor any of thy vows which thou vowest, nor thy voluntary-offerings, nor the heave-offering of thy hand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but before Jehovah thyGod shalt thou eat them in the place which Jehovah thyGod will choose, thou and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy bondman, and thy handmaid, and the Levite that is within thy gates; and thou shalt rejoice before Jehovah thyGod in all the business of thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed to thyself that thou forsake not the Levite all the days thou shalt be in thy land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Jehovah thyGod shall enlarge thy border, as he promised thee, and thou say, I will eat flesh, because thy soul longeth to eat flesh, thou mayest eat flesh, according to all the desire of thy soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the place which Jehovah thyGod will choose to set his name there be too far from thee, then thou shalt slay of thy kine and of thy sheep which Jehovah hath given thee, as I have commanded thee, and thou shalt eat in thy gates according to all the desire of thy soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even as the gazelle and the hart is eaten, so thou shalt eat them: the unclean and the clean alike may eat of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only, be sure that thou eat not the blood; for the blood is the life, and thou mayest not eat the life with the flesh;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou shalt not eat it; thou shalt pour it upon the earth as water:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou shalt not eat it; that it may go well with thee, and with thy children after thee, when thou shalt do what is right in the eyes of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thy hallowed things which thou hast, and what thou hast vowed, thou shalt take, and come to the place which Jehovah will choose;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt offer thy burnt-offerings, the flesh and the blood, upon the altar of Jehovah thyGod; and the blood of thy sacrifices shall be poured out upon the altar of Jehovah thyGod, and the flesh shalt thou eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed to hear all these words which I command thee, that it may be well with thee, and with thy children after thee for ever, when thou doest what is good and right in the eyes of Jehovah thyGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Jehovah thyGod cutteth off from before thee the nations whither thou goest, to take possession of them, and thou hast dispossessed them, and dwellest in their land,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: take heed to thyself that thou be not ensnared [to follow] after them, after that they are destroyed from before thee; and that thou inquire not after theirgods, saying, How did these nations serve theirgods? even so will I do likewise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not do so to Jehovah thyGod; for every [thing that is] abomination to Jehovah, which he hateth, have they done unto theirgods; for even their sons and their daughters have they burned in the fire to theirgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Everything that I command you, ye shall take heed to do it; thou shalt not add thereto, nor take from it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If there arise among you a prophet, or one that dreameth dreams, and he give thee a sign or a wonder,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the sign or the wonder come to pass that he told unto thee, when he said, Let us go after othergods, whom thou hast not known, and let us serve them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — thou shalt not hearken unto the words of that prophet, or that dreamer of dreams; for Jehovah yourGod proveth you, to know whether ye love Jehovah yourGod with all your heart and with all your soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall walk after Jehovah yourGod, and ye shall fear him, and his commandments shall ye keep, and his voice shall ye hear; and ye shall serve him, and unto him shall ye cleave.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that prophet, or that dreamer of dreams, shall be put to death; for he hath spoken revolt against Jehovah yourGod who brought you out of the land of Egypt, and redeemed you out of the house of bondage, — to draw thee out of the way that Jehovah thyGod commanded thee to walk in; and thou shalt put evil away from thy midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thy brother, the son of thy mother, or thy son, or thy daughter, or the wife of thy bosom, or thy friend, who is to thee as thy soul, entice thee secretly, saying, Let us go and serve othergods (whom thou hast not known, thou, nor thy fathers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of thegods of the peoples which are round about you, near unto thee, or far from thee, from one end of the earth even unto the other end of the earth),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou shalt not consent unto him, nor hearken unto him; neither shall thine eye spare him, neither shalt thou pity him, neither shalt thou screen him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but thou shalt in any case kill him: thy hand shall be the first against him to put him to death, and afterwards the hands of all the people;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt stone him with stones, that he die; for he hath sought to draw thee away from Jehovah thyGod who brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all Israel shall hear, and fear, and shall do no more any such wicked thing as this in thy midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If in one of thy cities, which Jehovah thyGod hath given thee to dwell there, thou hearest, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There are men, children of Belial, gone out from among you, and they have drawn away the inhabitants of their city, saying, Let us go and serve othergods, whom ye have not known;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then shalt thou inquire, and make search, and ask diligently; and if it be truth, [and] the thing be certain, that this abomination hath happened in the midst of thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou shalt surely smite the inhabitants of that city with the edge of the sword, devoting it to destruction, and all that is therein, and the cattle thereof, with the edge of the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the spoil of it shalt thou gather into the midst of the open place thereof, and shalt burn the city with fire, and all the spoil thereof, wholly to Jehovah thyGod; and it shall be a heap for ever; it shall not be built again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
