translations.text: And Moses called to all Israel, and said unto them, Ye have seen all that Jehovah did before your eyes in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh, and to all his bondmen, and to all his land:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the great trials that thine eyes have seen, those great signs and wonders.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah hath not given you a heart to perceive, and eyes to see, and ears to hear, to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have led you forty years in the wilderness; your clothes are not grown old upon you, and thy sandal is not grown old upon thy foot;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ye have not eaten bread, neither have ye drunk wine or strong drink, that ye might know that I am Jehovah yourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye came to this place; and Sihon the king of Heshbon and Og the king of Bashan came out against us for battle, and we smote them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we took their land, and gave it for an inheritance to the Reubenites, and to the Gadites, and to the half tribe of the Manassites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall keep then the words of this covenant, and do them, that ye may prosper in all that ye do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye stand this day all of you before Jehovah yourGod: your chiefs [of] your tribes, your elders, and your officers, all the men of Israel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: your little ones, your wives, and thy stranger that is in thy camp, as well the hewer of thy wood as the drawer of thy water;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that thou mayest enter into the covenant of Jehovah thyGod, and into his oath, which Jehovah thyGod maketh with thee this day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that he may establish thee this day for a people unto himself, and [that] he may be to thee aGod, as he hath said unto thee, and as he hath sworn unto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither with you only do I make this covenant and this oath,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but with him that standeth here with us this day before Jehovah ourGod, and with him that is not here with us this day

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for ye know how we dwelt in the land of Egypt, and how we came through the nations which ye passed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye have seen their abominations, and their idols, wood and stone, silver and gold, which were among them);

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest there should be among you man, or woman, or family, or tribe, whose heart turneth away this day from Jehovah ourGod, to go and serve thegods of these nations; lest there should be among you a root that beareth gall and wormwood,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and it come to pass, when he heareth the words of this curse, that he bless himself in his heart, saying, I shall have peace, though I walk in the stubbornness of my heart, to sweep away the drunken with the thirsty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah will not pardon him, but the anger of Jehovah and his jealousy will then smoke against that man, and all the curse shall be upon him that is written in this book; and Jehovah will blot out his name from under the heavens;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jehovah will separate him for mischief out of all the tribes of Israel, according to all the curses of the covenant that is written in this book of the law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the generation to come, your children who shall rise up after you, and the foreigner that shall come from a far land, shall say, when they see the plagues of that land, and its sicknesses wherewith Jehovah hath visited it,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [that] the whole ground thereof is brimstone and salt, [and] burning, that it is not sown, nor beareth, and no grass groweth in it, like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboim, which Jehovah overthrew in his anger and in his fury:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: even all nations shall say, Why has Jehovah done thus to this land? whence the heat of this great anger?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And men shall say, Because they have forsaken the covenant of Jehovah theGod of their fathers, which he had made with them when he brought them forth out of the land of Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they went and served othergods, and bowed down to them,gods whom they knew not, and whom he had not assigned to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the anger of Jehovah was kindled against this land, to bring upon it all the curse that is written in this book;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jehovah rooted them out of their land in anger, and in fury, and in great indignation, and cast them into another land, as [it appears] this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The hidden things belong to Jehovah ourGod; but the revealed ones are ours and our children's for ever, to do all the words of this law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when all these things are come upon thee, the blessing and the curse, which I have set before thee, and thou shalt take them to heart among all the nations whither Jehovah thyGod hath driven thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shalt return to Jehovah thyGod, and shalt hearken to his voice according to all that I command thee this day, thou and thy sons, with all thy heart and with all thy soul;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that then Jehovah thyGod will turn thy captivity, and have compassion upon thee, and will gather thee again from all the peoples whither Jehovah thyGod hath scattered thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though there were of you driven out unto the end of the heavens, from thence will Jehovah thyGod gather thee, and from thence will he fetch thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jehovah thyGod will bring thee into the land that thy fathers possessed, and thou shalt possess it; and he will do thee good, and multiply thee above thy fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah thyGod will circumcise thy heart, and the heart of thy seed, to love Jehovah thyGod with all thy heart and with all thy soul, that thou mayest live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah thyGod will put all these curses on thine enemies, and on them that hate thee, who have persecuted thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou shalt return and hearken to the voice of Jehovah, and do all his commandments which I command thee this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah thyGod will make thee abound in every work of thy hand, in the fruit of thy body, and in the fruit of thy cattle, and in the fruit of thy ground, for good; for Jehovah will again rejoice over thee for good, as he rejoiced over thy fathers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if thou shalt hearken unto the voice of Jehovah thyGod, to keep his commandments and his statutes which are written in this book of the law; if thou turn to Jehovah thyGod with all thy heart and with all thy soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this commandment which I command thee this day is not too wonderful for thee, neither is it far off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is not in the heavens, that thou shouldest say, Who shall go up for us to the heavens, and bring it to us, that we should hear it and do it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it is not beyond the sea, that thou shouldest say, Who shall go over the sea for us, and bring it to us, that we should hear it and do it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the word is very near to thee, in thy mouth and in thy heart, that thou mayest do it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See, I have set before thee this day life and good, and death and evil,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in that I command thee this day to love Jehovah thyGod, to walk in his ways, and to keep his commandments and his statutes and his ordinances, that thou mayest live and multiply, and that Jehovah thyGod may bless thee in the land whither thou goest to possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if thy heart turn away, so that thou wilt not hear, but shalt be drawn away, and thou shalt bow down to othergods and serve them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I denounce unto you this day that ye shall surely perish; ye shall not prolong your days upon the land whereunto thou passest over the Jordan to possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I call heaven and earth to witness this day against you: life and death have I set before you, blessing and cursing: choose then life, that thou mayest live, thou and thy seed,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in loving Jehovah thyGod, in hearkening to his voice, and in cleaving to him — for this is thy life and the length of thy days — that thou mayest dwell in the land which Jehovah swore unto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went and spoke these words to all Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he said unto them, I am a hundred and twenty years old this day, I can no more go out and come in; and Jehovah hath said unto me, Thou shalt not go over this Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah thyGod, he will go over before thee, he will destroy these nations from before thee, that thou mayest take possession of them: Joshua, he shall go over before thee, as Jehovah hath said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will do to them as he did to Sihon and to Og, the kings of the Amorites, and to their land; whom he destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jehovah giveth them up before you, ye shall do to them according to all the commandment which I have commanded you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be strong and courageous, fear them not, neither be afraid of them; for Jehovah thyGod, he it is that goeth with thee; he will not leave thee, nor forsake thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses called to Joshua, and said to him in the sight of all Israel, Be strong and courageous, for thou must go with this people into the land which Jehovah hath sworn unto their fathers to give them; and thou shalt cause them to inherit it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah, he it is that goeth before thee: he will be with thee; he will not leave thee, nor forsake thee; fear not, neither be dismayed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses wrote this law, and delivered it to the priests, the sons of Levi, who bore the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, and to all the elders of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses commanded them, saying, At the end of every seven years, at the set time of the year of release, at the feast of tabernacles,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when all Israel cometh to appear before Jehovah thyGod in the place which he will choose, thou shalt read this law before all Israel in their ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gather the people together, the men, and the women, and the children, and thy stranger that is within thy gates, that they may hear, and that they may learn, and fear Jehovah yourGod, and take heed to do all the words of this law;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and that their children who do not know it may hear it and learn, that they may fear Jehovah yourGod, as long as ye live in the land, whereunto ye pass over the Jordan to possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Lo, the days are near for thee to die; call Joshua, and present yourselves at the tent of meeting, that I may give him a charge. And Moses and Joshua went and presented themselves at the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah appeared at the tent in the pillar of cloud; and the pillar of cloud stood over the entrance to the tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Behold, thou shalt sleep with thy fathers; and this people will rise up, and go a whoring after the strangegods of the land into which they enter, and will forsake me, and break my covenant which I have made with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my anger shall be kindled against them in that day, and I will forsake them, and I will hide my face from them, and they shall be devoured, and many evils and troubles shall befall them, and they will say in that day, Have not these evils befallen me because myGod is not in my midst?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will entirely hide my face in that day for all the evils that they have wrought, because they turned unto othergods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, write ye this song, and teach it to the children of Israel; put it in their mouths, that this song may be a witness for me against the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I shall bring them into the land which I swore unto their fathers, which floweth with milk and honey; and they will eat and fill themselves, and wax fat, and will turn unto othergods, and serve them, and despise me, and break my covenant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when many evils and troubles have befallen them, that this song shall testify against them as a witness; for it shall not be forgotten out of the mouths of their seed; for I know their imagination which they are forming already this day, before I bring them into the land which I have sworn [unto them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses wrote this song the same day, and taught it to the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [Jehovah] commanded Joshua the son of Nun, and said, Be strong and courageous; for thou shalt bring the children of Israel into the land which I have sworn unto them; and I will be with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Moses had ended writing the words of this law in a book, until their conclusion,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that Moses commanded the Levites, who bore the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take this book of the law, and put it at the side of the ark of the covenant of Jehovah yourGod, that it may be there for a witness against thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I know thy rebellion, and thy stiff neck. Lo, while I am yet alive with you this day, ye have been rebellious against Jehovah; and how much more after my death!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gather to me all the elders of your tribes, and your officers, that I may speak these words in their ears, and take heaven and earth to witness against them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I know that after my death ye will utterly corrupt yourselves, and will turn aside from the way which I have commanded you; and mischief will befall you at the end of days; because ye do evil in the sight of Jehovah, to provoke him to anger through the work of your hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses spoke in the ears of the whole congregation of Israel the words of this song, until their conclusion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give ear, ye heavens, and I will speak; And hear, O earth, the words of my mouth!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My doctrine shall drop as rain, My speech flow down as dew, As small rain upon the tender herb, And as showers on the grass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the name of Jehovah will I proclaim: Ascribe greatness unto ourGod!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [He is] the Rock, his work is perfect, For all his ways are righteousness; AGod of faithfulness without deceit, Just and right is he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have dealt corruptly with him; Not his children's is their spot: — A crooked and perverted generation!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye thus requite Jehovah, Foolish and unwise people? Is not he thy father that hath bought thee? Hath he not made thee and established thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember the days of old, Consider the years of generation to generation; Ask thy father, and he will shew thee; Thine elders, and they will tell thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the Most High assigned to the nations their inheritance, When he separated the sons of Adam, He set the bounds of the peoples According to the number of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah's portion is his people; Jacob the lot of his inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He found him in a desert land, And in the waste, howling wilderness; He compassed him about, he watched over him, He preserved him as the apple of his eye.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As the eagle stirreth up its nest, Hovereth over its young, Spreadeth out its wings, Taketh them, beareth them on its feathers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So Jehovah alone did lead him, And no strangegod [was] with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He made him ride on the high places of the earth, And he ate the produce of the field; And he made him suck honey out of the crag, And oil out of the flinty rock;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cream of kine, and milk of sheep, With the fat of lambs, And rams of the breed of Bashan, and he-goats, With the fat of kidneys of wheat; And thou didst drink pure wine, the blood of the grape.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jeshurun grew fat, and kicked — Thou art waxen fat, Thou art grown thick, And thou art covered with fatness; — He gave upGod who made him, And lightly esteemed the Rock of his salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They moved him to jealousy with strangegods, With abominations did they provoke him to anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They sacrificed unto demons who are notGod; Togods whom they knew not, To new ones, who came newly up, Whom your fathers revered not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the Rock that begot thee wast thou unmindful, And thou hast forgottenGod who brought thee forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah saw it, and despised them, Because of the provoking of his sons and of his daughters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I will hide my face from them, I will see what their end shall be; For they are a perverse generation, Children in whom is no faithfulness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have moved me to jealousy with that which is noGod; They have exasperated me with their vanities; And I will move them to jealousy with that which is not a people; With a foolish nation will I provoke them to anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a fire is kindled in mine anger, And it shall burn into the lowest Sheol, And shall consume the earth and its produce, And set fire to the foundations of the mountains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will heap mischiefs upon them; Mine arrows will I spend against them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall be consumed with hunger, and devoured with burning heat, And with poisonous pestilence; And the teeth of beasts will I send against them, With the poison of what crawleth in the dust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From without shall the sword bereave them, and in the chambers, terror — Both the young man and the virgin, The suckling with the man of gray hairs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I would say, I will scatter, I will make the remembrance of them to cease from among men,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I did not fear provocation from the enemy, Lest their adversaries should misunderstand it, Lest they should say, Our hand is high, and Jehovah has not done all this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they are a nation void of counsel, And understanding is not in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that they had been wise! they would have understood this, They would have considered their latter end!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How could one chase a thousand, And two put ten thousand to flight, Were it not that their Rock had sold them, And Jehovah had delivered them up?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For their rock is not as our Rock: Let our enemies themselves be judges.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For their vine is of the vine of Sodom, And of the fields of Gomorrah: Their grapes are grapes of poison, Bitter are their clusters;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their wine is the poison of dragons, And the cruel venom of vipers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not this hidden with me, Sealed up among my treasures?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Vengeance is mine, and recompense, For the time when their foot shall slip. For the day of their calamity is at hand, And the things that shall come upon them make haste.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah will judge his people, And shall repent in favour of his servants; When he seeth that power is gone, And there is none shut up or left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall say, Where are theirgods, Their rock in whom they trusted,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who ate the fat of their sacrifices, [And] drank the wine of their drink-offering? Let them rise up and help you, That there may be a protection over you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See now that I, I am HE, And there is nogod with me; I kill, and I make alive; I wound, and I heal, And there is none that delivereth out of my hand,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I lift up my hand to the heavens, and say, I live for ever!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have sharpened my gleaming sword, And my hand take hold of judgment, I will render vengeance to mine adversaries, And will recompense them that hate me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine arrows will I make drunk with blood, And my sword shall devour flesh; [I will make them drunk] with the blood of the slain and of the captives, With the head of the princes of the enemy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shout for joy, ye nations, with his people, For he avengeth the blood of his servants, And rendereth vengeance to his enemies, And maketh atonement for his land, for his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses came and spoke all the words of this song in the ears of the people, he and Hoshea the son of Nun.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Moses had ended speaking all these words to all Israel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he said unto them, Set your hearts unto all the words that I testify among you this day, which ye shall command your children to take heed to do, all the words of this law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is no vain word for you, but it is your life, and through this word ye shall prolong your days on the land whereunto ye pass over the Jordan to possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses that same day, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go up into this mountain Abarim, mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, which is opposite Jericho; and behold the land of Canaan, which I give unto the children of Israel for a possession,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and die on the mountain whither thou goest up, and be gathered unto thy peoples, as Aaron thy brother died on mount Hor, and was gathered unto his peoples;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because ye trespassed against me among the children of Israel at the waters of Meribah-Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin; because ye hallowed me not in the midst of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou shalt see the land before [thee]; but thou shalt not go thither unto the land which I give the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the blessing, wherewith Moses the man ofGod blessed the children of Israel before his death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Jehovah came from Sinai, And rose up from Seir unto them; He shone forth from mount Paran, And he came from the myriads of the sanctuary; From his right hand [went forth] a law of fire for them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, he loveth the peoples, All his saints are in thy hand, And they sit down at thy feet; Each receiveth of thy words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moses commanded us a law, The inheritance of the congregation of Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was king in Jeshurun, When the heads of the people And the tribes of Israel were gathered together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let Reuben live, and not die; And let his men be few.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this of Judah; and he said, Hear, Jehovah, the voice of Judah, And bring him unto his people; May his hands strive for them; And be thou a help to him against his oppressors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Levi he said, Thy Thummim and thy Urim are for thygodly one, Whom thou didst prove at Massah, With whom thou didst strive at the waters of Meribah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who said to his father and to his mother, I see him not, And he acknowledged not his brethren, And knew not his own children; For they have observed thy word, And kept thy covenant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall teach Jacob thine ordinances, And Israel thy law: They shall put incense before thy nostrils, And whole burnt-offering upon thine altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bless, Jehovah, his substance! And let the work of his hands please thee; Crush the loins of his adversaries, And of them that hate him, that they may never rise again!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of Benjamin he said, The beloved of Jehovah, — he shall dwell in safety by him; He will cover him all the day long, And dwell between his shoulders.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Joseph he said, Blessed of Jehovah be his land! By the precious things of the heavens, By the dew, and by the deep that lieth beneath,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by the precious fruits of the sun, And by the precious things put forth by the months,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by the best things of the ancient mountains, And by the precious things of the everlasting hills,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by the precious things of the earth and the fulness thereof. And let the good will of him that dwelt in the bush Come upon the head of Joseph, Upon the top of the head of him that was separated from his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His majesty is as the firstling of his ox; And his horns are as the horns of a buffalo. With them shall he push the peoples Together to the ends of the earth. These are the myriads of Ephraim, And these are the thousands of Manasseh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Zebulun he said, Rejoice, Zebulun, in thy going out; And thou, Issachar, in thy tents!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall invite [the] peoples to the mountain; There they shall offer sacrifices of righteousness; For they will suck the abundance of the seas, And the hidden treasures of the sand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Gad he said, Blessed be he that enlargeth Gad! As a lion doth he dwell, and teareth the arm, even the top of the head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he provided the first part for himself, For there was reserved the portion of the lawgiver; And he came with the heads of the people; The justice of Jehovah and his judgments Hath he executed with Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Dan he said, Dan is a young lion; He shall spring forth from Bashan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Naphtali he said, Naphtali, satisfied with favour, And full of the blessing of Jehovah, Possess thou the west and the south.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Asher he said, Asher shall be blessed with sons; Let him be acceptable to his brethren, And let him dip his foot in oil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Iron and brass shall be thy bolts; And thy rest as thy days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is none like unto theGod of Jeshurun, Who rideth upon the heavens to thy help, And in his majesty, upon the clouds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Thy] refuge is theGod of old, And underneath are the eternal arms; And he shall drive out the enemy from before thee, And shall say, Destroy [them]!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel shall dwell in safety alone, The fountain of Jacob, in a land of corn and new wine; Also his heavens shall drop down dew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Happy art thou, Israel! Who is like unto thee, a people saved by Jehovah, The shield of thy help, And the sword of thine excellency? And thine enemies shall come cringing to thee; And thou shalt tread upon their high places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went up from the plains of Moab to mount Nebo, to the top of Pisgah, which is opposite Jericho. And Jehovah shewed him the whole land, Gilead to Dan,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all Naphtali, and the land of Ephraim, and Manasseh, and all the land of Judah, unto the hindmost sea,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the south, and the plain of the valley of Jericho, the city of palm-trees, to Zoar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto him, This is the land that I swore unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed will I give it: I have caused thee to see it with thine eyes, but thou shalt not go over thither.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses the servant of Jehovah died there in the land of Moab, according to the word of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he buried him in the valley in the land of Moab, opposite Beth-Peor; and no man knows his sepulchre to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses was a hundred and twenty years old when he died; his eye was not dim, nor his natural force abated.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel wept for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days; and the days of weeping and mourning for Moses were ended.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua the son of Nun was filled with the spirit of wisdom, for Moses had laid his hands upon him; and the children of Israel hearkened unto him, and did as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there arose no prophet since in Israel like Moses, whom Jehovah had known face to face;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: according to all the signs and wonders that Jehovah had sent him to do in the land of Egypt, to Pharaoh, and to all his servants, and to all his land;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and according to all that mighty hand; and according to all the great terribleness that Moses had wrought in the sight of all Israel.
